NOTIRCATION New Delh, the 24th October, 2008 GSR. 7S2(E)—WHEREAS, the Central Government has fixed noise limits for generator sets run with diesel manufaciured on or ofter the 19 Januaty, 2005 to provide acousfic enclosure at manufacturing stage to ensure maximum permissible sound pressure level at 75 dB(A) ot 1.0 metre from the enclosure surface, by moking further amendments in the Environment (Protection) Rules, 1986; AND WHEREAS, the Central Government has received representations from various Manufacturers and their Associations requesting for certain amendments in view of the various difficulties faced by the assembiers of DG Sefs in small scale sector i.e. . definiion of manufacturer and the transportation of DG sets; AND WHEREAS, the Central Government, in exercise of the powers conferred by clause {iii) of sub-section {2 of section 3 and section 25 of the Environment [Protection} Act, 1986 (29 of 1986) further to amend the Environment (Protection) Rules, 1986, published the draft rules in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, vide G.S.R. 704 (E}, dated 12 November, 2007 for the information of ak persons fikely to be affected thereby; and nofice was given that the said draft rules would be taken into -consideration by the Central Government on or after the expiry of a period of sixty days from the date on which copies of the Gazette centaining this notification were made available to the public: AND WHEREAS, the copies of the said Gazette nofification were made available 1o the public on the 24th November, 2007; AND WHEREAS, al objecfions and suggestions received in response to the above menlioned draft notification have been duly considered by the Central Government; NQOW, THEREFORE, in exercise of the powers conferred by secfion 6 and 25 of the Environment {Protection} Act. 1986 the Ceniral Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Environment {Protection) Rules, 1986, namely:- 1. (1} These rules may be called the Environment [Protection) Eighth Amendment Rules, 2008. -- 4 of 6 -- [ymil-—avg 3] W AT : SR 2) They shall come inte force on the date of their pubiication in the Official Gczetie. 2. In the Environment (Protection) Rules, 1986, in Schedule |,- {a)in serial nUmber 94 relating to 'NOISE LIMIT FOR GENERATOR SETS RUN WITH DIESEL", in paragroph 3.2+ i) for the words ‘Every manufacturer or impoiter theseinafter referred to as “supplier”)’, the words ‘Every manufacturer orf assembier or importer [hereinafter referred to as the “manufacturer”)’ shall be substituted; fif for the words ‘manufactured or imported'. the words ‘manufactured or assembled or imported' shall be substituted; {b) in paragraph 3.4, in item {i} and item (i} (), for the word “suppiier”, the word "manufacturer” shall be substituted; (¢} in paragraph 4.0, in item (i), for the word “supplier”. the word “manufacturer” shall be substituted; {d) after paragraph 50, the forlowmg paragraph shal be inserted, namely:- 6.0 Transportation of Diesel Genercitor Sets {above 250 KVA) (i) Diesel Generator set shall be ransported after fulfiling the requirement of certification specified in paragraph 3.2 as o complete unit with acoustic enclosure, or dismantled, with relevant genset number specified on acoustic enciosure and silencer for reassembling at the site of its operation. (I} Compliance with the noise noims shall be monitored after reassembling the D.G. set af the location of the installation by the concemed State Pollution Control Board or, as the case may be, the Union Tewitory . Pollution Control Committee.” ; / [F.
